response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial domain knowledge through GISCollective and demonstrated high-performance systems expertise, the role requires immediate C++ productivity at senior level. Professional C++ work ended over a decade ago (2014-2015), with recent focus on TypeScript/Node.js. Most critically, there is no evidence of building navigation routing systems—the core technical responsibility. The gap between current skills and role requirements is too large for a mid-senior individual contributor.
strengths: Extensive geospatial domain knowledge from founding and operating GISCollective, a production geo-data platform currently used by organizations like Green Map
Demonstrated high-performance systems expertise at Dunnhumby with async I/O, caching, fibers, and microservices handling millions of requests per second
Proven adaptability across multiple languages, platforms, and problem domains, showing the willingness to expand expertise that the role emphasizes
concerns: Professional C++ experience is over 10 years old (National Instruments 2014-2015); recent 1.5+ years entirely in TypeScript/Node.js, moving away from systems programming
No navigation or routing system experience; GIS background is in data collection and mapping tools, not routing engines, pathfinding, or tile optimization
No Rust experience as fallback, and no background in data pipelines, real-time traffic integration, map tiling, or navigation-specific infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Geospatial knowledge and production systems background
